title: Barnebydendron riedelii
taxonomy: Barnebydendron riedelii is a flowering plant species in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to Brazil. The classification of this plant includes its kingdom as Plantae, and it belongs to the phylum Magnoliophyta. Within the class Magnoliopsida, the order Sapindales, and the family Sapindaceae are the other classification levels. Barnebydendron riedelii is also known by its synonym, Cupania riedelii.

intro: Barnebydendron riedelii, also known as the Riedel's tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Barnebydendron riedelii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as riedelin, which have been found to exhibit significant pharmacological activities. The plant is often used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
